evidence_excerpts: By the end of the lesson, learners will be able to distinguish between finite and infinite sets using practical examples from their environment.

evidence_excerpts: Concrete exploration of countable objects (stones) vs. uncountable quantities (sand). The learner identifies sets that have a limited number of elements versus those that do not. Practical counting of stones.; Attempting to count grains of sand.; Identifying which count can be finished.; Group activity with local materials.

evidence_excerpts: Definition of Finite (limited members) and Infinite (unlimited members) sets. The learner defines finite and infinite sets correctly. Finite Set: Has a countable number of members.; Infinite Set: Has members that go on forever.; Examples from daily life.
